transcript.clean_text: they all Den Hills in the southeast of Belgium play host to one of the hardest tests in professional cycling it's the oldest of the big Classics the old lady where the young Pretenders turning up to try and win s Remco for the first time since the world championships and a world champion racing in Belgium his home nation for the first time this season 258.1 kilometers with 11 classified climbs lots of unclassified climbing a route South towards bastonia and then back towards liege the big city when the race finished after all the famous names Eddie Max's playground at the Stocker one through Spa and of course up to the mighty the famous Hill the iconic Hill in liege one climb returning this year back after the floods prevented it being in the race route the year previous by the time we had live images there was the news that tale pogachar was out of the race having crashed he was on his way to hospital with Michael Ray to check on his injuries well gacha would have a fracture to multiple bones in his wrist Quick Step still controlled as 11 men got up in the break with a maximum advantage of four and a half minutes they were soon whittled down and in the final 90ks a move launched by Jan tratnick valortan Madras tried to follow as did Magnus Sheffield but tragnik would in the end go alone and get across a gap that started off as just over a minute and a half with Emerick Mass suffering from stomach problems David godu too going backwards tratnik was on his way to the front of the race one by one teammates was sacrificed in the service of Remco ivnapol including Julian a la Felipe and the rest and as tratnik reached the front only five remained one by one two they'd be ridden off the slovenian's wheel the man from yombo visma having come down from altitude showing he was ready for the g-dop his teammates tried to help but it was still our Quick Step riding like motorbikes rather than them getting in the way 12 months previous tracknik would eventually be caught by the Lone Survivor from the break Simone Velasco and just before the top Eve Nepal coming off the wheel of Elon Von Wilder and attacking at the beginning Tom pidcock was able to follow Julio chikone wouldn't be far away a right turn instead of a left turn onto the false slat this year and a bit more climbing to come and it would be on that second section of climbing over the top of that despite Pitt got unable to give a turn if Nepal wouldn't worry too much he'd simply ride the British Rider off his wheel and with 29 kilometers remaining just like last year even the pool would leave the competition behind this time he'd be even more dominant in the rainbow jersey quickly getting half a minute and as they looked at each other to chase behind the Gap would balloon to over a minute the rain falling the cold everywhere the damage in all truth already done by even a pool fans began to celebrate his victory that was impending and the Gap would go over to a minute and a half on the final climb of the day into the final couple of kilometers and a chance to celebrate slippery descents already negotiated conservatively and into the final finish straight two appearances in liege two victories foreign the first man to go back to back in over a quarter of a century the first man in 37 years to win in the rainbow jersey Remco repeating this time as the world champion so now quick steps spring savior Santiago butrago with his best ever result in a world tour one day race he took third and the first Monument Podium for Tom pidcock who'd finish in second place even a pool beating Pitcock and Company by over a minute top 10 looking like this even Neapolitan and madwas in the top five second good weekend for Ben Healy who came close to the podium again what a Podium and what a rider Nepal looking every bit ready for the Giroud Italia that and other races coming soon and you'll better watch them all right here on the Homer cycling Eurosport Discovery plus and gcn plus
